data.content:
The bug is clear — `ProcessPayment` only checks `amount == 0` but not negative values. The fastest workflow is **`dotnet watch test`**, which auto-reruns tests on every file save.

Let me start it, then apply the fix:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

data.reasoningText:
The bug is clear: `ProcessPayment` only checks for `amount == 0` but doesn't check for negative amounts. The fix is to add a check for negative amounts.

The user wants the fastest workflow for iterating. The best approach is `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests automatically on file save.

Let me first show the fix and then set up `dotnet watch test`.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-e9710226a333426da3a0d681eaa7bfc7 && dotnet watch test --filter \"ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException\" 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for the failing test", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch-test"}

toolArgs.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test
